name: Pumpkin Light Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Granulated artificial sweetener OR 2 tablespoons Liquid Sugar Twin
recipeInstructions:
1. Leftover pie may be frozen.
2. Melt butter or margarine in a 9-inch pie plate at High for 30 to 40 seconds. Add Graham wafer crumbs to the pie plate and combine well with a fork. Spread evenly over the bottom of the pie plate.
3. Microwave at High for 3 to 5 minutes, watching carefully to prevent scorching. Allow to cool before filling.
4. Dissolve gelatin in cold water and set aside.
5. Beat egg yolks in a 2-quart measure or mixing bowl. Stir in milk, pumpkin, salt, and spices. Mix well.
6. Microwave at Medium Low (50%) for 30-second intervals until thick and smooth, stirring after each interval. Add gelatin and sweetener. Stir until completely dissolved.
7. Refrigerate until the thickness of unbeaten egg whites.
8. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar and continue beating until stiff and shiny.
9. Fold into pumpkin mixture, being careful to combine thoroughly. Pour into prepared pie shell.
10. Chill overnight.
1/8 pie - 168 calories, 16 grams carbohydrate, 5 grams protein, 8 grams fat, 1 starch, 1 protein, 1 fat & oils choice
Source: More Choice, the Canadian Diabetic Micro. Cookbook 1991 Catha McMaster & Charlotte Empringham
